name: Jump Squat
description: Explosive squat variation that builds lower body power and speed.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Lower into a squat until thighs are parallel to the floor
Explode upward and jump as high as possible
Land softly with bent knees
Immediately lower into the next rep

name: Bulgarian Pin Squat
description: Single-leg squat with rear foot elevated, targeting quads and glutes.
instructions: Place rear foot on a bench behind you, front foot forward
Stand upright with core engaged
Lower until front thigh is parallel to the floor
Push through front heel to return to start
Complete all reps on one side, then switch

name: Split Squat Calf Raises
description: Combines a split squat position with calf raises for lower leg strength.
instructions: Stand in a split squat stance, front foot forward
Lower into a split squat position and hold
Rise up onto the front toes in a calf raise
Lower the heel back down with control
Repeat for target reps, then switch sides

name: Sprints
description: Short, all-out running efforts to build speed and cardiovascular power.
instructions: Warm up with 5 minutes of light jogging
Drive knees high and pump arms aggressively
Sprint at 80–100% effort for 20–40 meters
Walk back to start for recovery
Repeat 6–10 times

name: Battle Ropes
description: Conditioning exercise using heavy ropes to build power and endurance.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, slight bend in knees
Hold one rope end in each hand
Alternately slam each rope up and down in a wave motion
Keep core tight and maintain a steady rhythm
Perform for 20–40 seconds per set

name: Med Ball Slams
description: Explosive full-body movement that develops power and conditions the whole body.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, hold med ball overhead
Rise onto toes and brace core
Slam the ball into the ground as hard as possible
Catch on the bounce or pick it up
Repeat for 8–12 reps

name: Med Ball Pushes
description: Power throw that trains explosive upper body and core strength.
instructions: Stand facing a wall, hold med ball at chest height
Step forward and explosively push the ball into the wall
Fully extend both arms at the point of release
Catch the rebound and immediately reset
Perform 8–12 explosive reps

name: Sled Pushes
description: Full-body conditioning movement that builds leg drive and work capacity.
instructions: Load the sled with your desired weight
Place hands on the handles and lean forward at about 45°
Drive through your legs in short, powerful steps
Keep core tight and back flat throughout
Push 20–40 meters, rest, then repeat

name: Sled Pulls
description: Pulling movement that builds posterior chain strength and conditioning.
instructions: Attach a harness or rope to the sled
Face away from the sled and lean forward slightly
Drive forward with powerful leg steps while the sled follows
Keep upright posture and engage core
Pull 20–40 meters, rest, then repeat

name: Boden Walks
description: Walking lunges performed across the floor for leg and glute endurance.
instructions: Stand upright with feet together
Step forward with one foot and lower back knee toward the floor
Keep front knee above ankle and torso upright
Push off back foot and step into the next lunge
Continue alternating legs across the floor

name: Wall Sit Holds
description: Static lower body hold that builds quad endurance and knee strength.
instructions: Stand with back flat against a wall
Slide down until thighs are parallel to the floor
Keep knees directly above ankles and feet flat
Hold the position without sliding
Aim for 30–60 seconds

name: Squat Holds
description: Static hold at the bottom of a squat to build quad and hip strength.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
Lower into a deep squat until thighs are parallel or below
Keep chest up, heels flat, and knees over toes
Hold the bottom position without bouncing
Aim for 20–60 seconds

name: Lunge Holds
description: Static lunge position that challenges quad, glute, and hip flexor strength.
instructions: Step one foot forward into a lunge stance
Lower back knee toward the floor without touching it
Keep front knee above ankle and torso upright
Hold with both legs engaged
Aim for 20–40 seconds per side

name: Single Leg Holds
description: Single-leg balance hold to strengthen stabilizers and improve balance.
instructions: Stand on one leg with a slight bend in the knee
Lift the opposite foot a few inches off the ground
Keep hips level and core engaged
Focus on a fixed point ahead to help balance
Hold for 30–60 seconds per side

name: Copenhagen Plank
description: Side plank variation with the top leg on a bench, targeting the adductors and core.
instructions: Place your top foot on a bench and let the bottom leg hang
Press into a side plank position with your elbow on the floor
Lift your hips until your body forms a straight line
Keep core tight and hips elevated
Hold for 15–30 seconds per side

name: Plank
description: Fundamental core exercise that builds full-body stability and endurance.
instructions: Start on forearms and toes, elbows directly below shoulders
Keep body in a straight line from head to heels
Brace core, squeeze glutes, and breathe steadily
Avoid letting hips sag or rise
Hold for 30–60 seconds

name: Side Plank
description: Lateral core exercise that strengthens the obliques and hip stabilizers.
instructions: Lie on your side with elbow below shoulder
Stack feet or stagger them for balance
Lift hips off the floor until body forms a straight line
Keep core tight and hips elevated throughout
Hold for 20–40 seconds per side

name: Glute Bridge Holds
description: Isometric glute hold at the top of a bridge position.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor
Drive hips upward by squeezing glutes until body forms a straight line
Keep core tight and avoid hyperextending the lower back
Hold the top position with glutes fully contracted
Aim for 20–40 seconds

name: Single Leg RDL Holds
description: Single-leg hinge hold that challenges hamstrings and hip stabilizers.
instructions: Stand on one leg with a slight bend in the knee
Hinge forward at the hip, extending the free leg behind you
Lower torso until roughly parallel to the floor, keep back flat
Hold with core tight and hips square
Aim for 15–30 seconds per side

name: Adductor Holds
description: Isometric inner thigh hold to strengthen the adductors.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and a ball between your knees
Squeeze the ball between your knees with maximal force
Keep lower back flat on the floor
Hold the squeeze without holding your breath
Aim for 20–40 seconds

name: Swings
description: Foundational kettlebell movement that develops explosive hip power and posterior chain strength.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, kettlebell between feet
Hinge at hips and grip the kettlebell with both hands
Hike the kettlebell back between your legs
Drive hips forward explosively to swing the bell to chest height
Let it swing back and repeat for 15–20 reps

name: Goblet Squat
description: Front-loaded squat holding the kettlebell at chest height for depth and posture.
instructions: Hold kettlebell by the horns at chest height
Stand with feet slightly wider than hip-width, toes out
Lower into a deep squat keeping elbows inside your knees
Keep chest up and heels flat on the floor
Drive through heels to stand — 10–15 reps

name: Single Leg Deadlift
description: Unilateral hip hinge with a kettlebell that strengthens hamstrings and balance.
instructions: Stand on one leg, holding the kettlebell in the opposite hand
Hinge forward at the hips, extending the free leg behind you
Lower the kettlebell toward the floor, keeping back flat
Drive through the standing heel to return upright
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Lunge
description: Kettlebell lunge that adds load for greater leg and glute development.
instructions: Hold a kettlebell in each hand hanging at sides or one in rack position
Step one foot forward and lower back knee toward the floor
Keep front knee above ankle and torso upright
Push through front heel to return to start
Perform 8–12 reps per side

name: Racked Squat
description: Squat with kettlebell in rack position that challenges core stability and leg strength.
instructions: Hold the kettlebell in rack position (resting on forearm, elbow tucked)
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
Lower into a squat keeping the bell stable and torso upright
Drive through heels to return to standing
Perform 8–12 reps per side

name: Clean Arm Press
description: Two-phase kettlebell movement combining a clean and a press for power and strength.
instructions: Hinge at hips with kettlebell between legs
Drive hips forward and pull the bell into rack position (the clean)
From rack, press the bell overhead until arm is fully extended
Lower back to rack, then to starting position
Perform 6–8 reps per side

name: Shoulder Presses
description: Overhead press with kettlebell for shoulder and upper body strength.
instructions: Hold kettlebell in rack position at shoulder height
Brace core and keep elbow tucked below the bell
Press the bell straight overhead until arm is fully extended
Lower slowly back to rack position
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Rows
description: Single-arm kettlebell row that builds upper back and bicep strength.
instructions: Place one hand and knee on a bench for support
Hold kettlebell in the other hand, arm extended toward floor
Pull the bell toward your hip, driving elbow back
Keep back flat and avoid rotating the torso
Lower with control — 8–12 reps per side

name: Windmills
description: Complex movement that develops shoulder stability, hip mobility, and core strength.
instructions: Press kettlebell overhead with one arm, feet shoulder-width apart
Turn feet 45° away from the arm holding the bell
Hinge sideways at the hip, lowering the free hand toward the floor
Keep eyes on the kettlebell throughout
Return to standing — 5–8 reps per side

name: Alternating Swings
description: Single-arm swing variation that adds rotational challenge and grip training.
instructions: Perform a standard two-handed swing to chest height
At the top, release with one hand and catch with the other
Let the bell swing back between your legs
Drive hips forward and repeat the hand switch each rep
Perform 10–15 reps per side

name: Squats
description: Fundamental lower body exercise targeting quads, glutes, and hamstrings.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
Brace core and push hips back as you lower down
Lower until thighs are parallel to the floor or deeper
Keep chest up and heels flat on the floor
Drive through heels to return to standing — 8–12 reps

name: Lunges
description: Unilateral leg exercise for quad, glute, and balance development.
instructions: Stand with feet together, hands on hips or holding weights
Step one foot forward and lower back knee toward the floor
Keep front knee above ankle and torso upright
Push off front foot to return to starting position
Alternate legs for 10–12 reps per side

name: RDL's
description: Hip hinge movement targeting the hamstrings and glutes with a slight knee bend.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding weights in front of thighs
Push hips back and lower the weights along your legs
Stop when you feel a strong hamstring stretch
Keep back flat and a slight bend in the knees
Drive hips forward to return — 8–12 reps

name: Lat Pull Downs
description: Cable exercise that builds upper back width by training the latissimus dorsi.
instructions: Sit at a lat pulldown machine, grip bar wider than shoulder-width
Lean slightly back and pull the bar down to upper chest level
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the bottom
Extend arms slowly to return to start
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Bankdrücken
description: Barbell bench press — the fundamental compound chest exercise.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with eyes below the bar
Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder-width
Unrack and lower bar to mid-chest with control
Press bar back up to starting position
Perform 5–8 reps — use a spotter with heavy weight

name: Shoulder Press
description: Overhead pressing movement for overall shoulder and upper body strength.
instructions: Hold dumbbells or barbell at shoulder height, palms facing forward
Brace core and press weights straight overhead
Fully extend arms at the top
Lower with control back to shoulder level
Perform 8–12 reps

name: Lateral Raises
description: Shoulder isolation targeting the medial deltoid for shoulder width.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides, slight bend in elbows
Raise both arms to the sides until they reach shoulder height
Keep wrists neutral and avoid shrugging
Lower with control back to sides
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Curls
description: Classic bicep curl for building arm size and elbow flexor strength.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides with palms facing forward
Curl both weights toward your shoulders by bending at the elbow
Squeeze biceps at the top
Lower with control back to starting position
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Tricep Push Downs
description: Cable exercise for tricep isolation by pushing the attachment downward.
instructions: Stand at a cable machine with rope or bar attachment at head height
Grip with both hands, elbows tucked at sides
Push the attachment down until arms are fully extended
Squeeze triceps at the bottom, then return slowly
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Crunches
description: Classic ab exercise that targets the rectus abdominis.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat, hands behind head
Brace core and curl head and shoulders off the floor
Exhale at the top, do not pull on your neck
Lower with control back to start
Perform 15–20 reps

name: Butterfly
description: Hip-opening stretch targeting the inner thighs and hip flexors.
instructions: Sit on the floor and bring the soles of your feet together
Let knees drop out to the sides
Hold your feet and sit tall with a straight back
Gently press knees toward the floor
Hold for 30–60 seconds

name: Butterfly Holds
description: Isometric hold at the butterfly position to deepen the hip-opening stretch.
instructions: Sit in butterfly position with soles of feet together
Place hands on knees and gently press them toward the floor
Hold at the point of mild resistance
Breathe steadily and relax into the position
Hold for 30–60 seconds

name: Couch Stretch
description: Hip flexor and quad stretch performed against a wall or couch.
instructions: Kneel with one shin against a wall or couch behind you
Step the other foot forward into a lunge position
Drive hips forward and upright until you feel the hip flexor stretch
Keep torso tall and core lightly engaged
Hold for 60–90 seconds per side

name: Wrist Circles
description: Mobility drill for the wrists to improve range of motion and joint health.
instructions: Extend arms in front at shoulder height
Make a loose fist with both hands
Rotate wrists in large circles — 10 rotations clockwise
Reverse direction for 10 rotations counter-clockwise
Repeat 2–3 times

name: Cobra
description: Spinal extension stretch that opens the chest and stretches the abs.
instructions: Lie face down with hands under shoulders, elbows bent
Press palms into the floor and lift your chest up
Keep hips on the floor and elbows slightly bent
Look straight ahead and relax the lower back
Hold for 20–30 seconds

name: Cat Cow
description: Spinal mobility drill alternating between flexion and extension.
instructions: Start on hands and knees, wrists below shoulders, knees below hips
Inhale and drop belly toward floor, lifting head and tailbone (Cow)
Exhale and round spine toward the ceiling, tucking chin and pelvis (Cat)
Move fluidly between the two positions
Repeat for 10–15 slow reps

name: Child Pose
description: Resting stretch that opens the hips and releases lower back tension.
instructions: Kneel on the floor and sit back toward your heels
Reach arms forward and lower your forehead to the floor
Let the chest sink toward the floor
Breathe deeply into the back
Hold for 30–60 seconds

name: 90-90 Stretch
description: Hip mobility drill targeting internal and external rotation in both hips.
instructions: Sit with both knees bent at 90° — one leg in front, one to the side
Keep torso upright and both sit bones on the floor
Lean gently forward over the front shin to feel the stretch
Hold for 30–45 seconds, then switch sides
Repeat 2–3 times per side

name: Seated Good Mornings
description: Seated forward fold targeting hamstring flexibility and lower back mobility.
instructions: Sit on the floor with legs extended straight in front
Sit tall and brace core lightly
Hinge forward at the hips, reaching hands toward your feet
Keep back as flat as possible
Hold for 30–60 seconds

name: Pogo Jumps
description: Rapid small hops on the spot to train ankle stiffness and reactive strength.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, slight bend in knees
Jump using only ankle and calf spring — minimal knee bend
Land and immediately jump again with minimal ground contact time
Keep torso upright and arms relaxed
Perform 20–30 reps

name: Box Jumps
description: Explosive jump onto a box to develop lower body power and landing control.
instructions: Stand facing a sturdy box (30–60 cm)
Swing arms back and bend knees to load the jump
Explode upward and forward to land on the box with both feet
Land softly with bent knees in an athletic position
Step back down and repeat — 5–8 reps

name: Broad Jumps
description: Horizontal jump for maximum distance to train triple extension and power.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Load hips back and swing arms behind
Explode forward and upward, swinging arms forward for momentum
Land with both feet, soft knees in a balanced position
Perform 5–8 reps

name: Tuck Jumps
description: Explosive jump pulling knees to chest at peak height to develop power and coordination.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart
Jump as high as possible, pulling both knees toward the chest at the top
Release legs to land softly with bent knees
Absorb the landing and immediately repeat
Perform 8–10 reps

name: Burpees
description: Full-body conditioning movement combining a squat, plank, push-up, and jump.
instructions: Stand, then squat down and place hands on the floor
Jump or step feet back into a push-up position
Perform a push-up (optional)
Jump or step feet back to your hands and stand up
Jump with arms overhead to complete one rep — 10–15 reps

name: Clap Pushups
description: Push-up with a mid-air clap to develop upper body power and coordination.
instructions: Start in standard push-up position
Lower chest to the floor with control
Explode upward with enough power to clap hands mid-air
Catch yourself with soft elbows and lower for the next rep
Perform 5–8 reps

name: Split Jumps
description: Plyometric lunge jump that switches legs in the air for power and coordination.
instructions: Start in a lunge position with one foot forward
Jump upward explosively, switching leg positions in the air
Land in the opposite lunge position, absorbing impact softly
Immediately jump again to switch back
Perform 8–12 reps per side

name: Skater Jumps
description: Single-leg lateral jumps mimicking a speed skater to build lateral power.
instructions: Stand on one leg
Explode laterally to the other side, landing on the opposite single leg
Allow the trail leg to swing behind for balance
Absorb the landing and immediately push off to the other side
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Depth Drops
description: Step off a box and land softly to train impact absorption and stiffness.
instructions: Stand on a box (30–50 cm), toes at the edge
Step off (do not jump) and drop to the floor
Land with both feet simultaneously, absorbing with bent knees
Focus on a quiet, controlled landing
Perform 5–8 reps

name: Med Ball Slam
description: Full-body explosive throw of a med ball into the ground for power and conditioning.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, med ball overhead
Rise onto toes and brace core
Slam the ball into the ground with maximum force
Catch on the bounce or pick it up
Repeat for 8–12 reps

name: Russian Twists
description: Seated rotational core exercise targeting the obliques and deep core muscles.
instructions: Sit with knees bent and lean back to about 45°
Hold a weight at chest level with both hands
Rotate torso to one side, touching the weight toward the floor
Rotate to the other side to complete one rep
Perform 20 total reps (10 per side)

name: Trunk Rotation
description: Standing core rotation drill for spinal mobility and rotational strength.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, arms crossed or holding a stick
Rotate upper body to one side while keeping hips facing forward
Return to center and rotate to the other side
Control the movement, do not bounce at end range
Perform 15–20 reps per side

name: Alternating Plate Turns
description: Standing plate rotation that develops core anti-rotation and rotational power.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a weight plate at chest
Rotate to one side, extending arms straight out
Return to center and rotate to the other side
Keep hips facing forward and rotate only the torso
Perform 12–15 reps per side

name: Cable Woodchop
description: Diagonal cable pull from high to low that builds rotational core power.
instructions: Set cable at high position and stand sideways to the machine
Grip the handle with both hands above the shoulder
Pull the cable diagonally across the body to the opposite hip
Keep arms relatively straight and rotate through the core
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Med Ball Throws Alternating
description: Alternating rotational throws against a wall to build explosive core power.
instructions: Stand sideways to a wall, holding a med ball at hip level
Rotate away from the wall to load up
Rotate back explosively and throw the ball into the wall
Catch the rebound and immediately set up the next throw
Perform 10 reps per side

name: Shoulder Back Rotation
description: Rotational shoulder mobility drill to improve thoracic rotation and posture.
instructions: Stand or sit upright with arms extended to the sides at shoulder height
Rotate one arm forward and the other backward simultaneously
Keep core stable and movement controlled
Alternate directions fluidly
Perform 10–15 reps per side
